title: podman build concurrency: unable to cleanup run mounts error in copier subprocess
body:
### Issue Description

In a multistage concurrent build with stages A,B,C,D and B,C,D running concurrently depending on A, if any of B,C,D exit, it appears that they garbage collect A, which leads to confusing errors in the other dependent processes (File Not Found/etc) if any of B,C,D are writing to the filesystem.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a containerfile as follows:
```
FROM alpine AS A

FROM A AS B
RUN exit 1

FROM A AS C
RUN for i in $(seq 0 100); do echo "hello world ${i}" > file-${i}; done

FROM A AS D
RUN exit 0
```
3. Run `podman build . --no-cache --skip-unused-stages=false --jobs=0`
4. Notice that a race condition exists (~66%?) where you see issues like `"error unmounting container: unmounting build container \"\": layer not known"`, `File Not Found` in `containers/storage/overlay`, etc



### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:

```
[1/4] STEP 1/1: FROM alpine AS A
--> 05455a08881e
[2/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS B
[3/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS C
[4/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS D
[3/4] STEP 2/2: RUN for i in $(seq 0 100); do echo "hello world ${i}" > file-${i}; done
[2/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 1
[4/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 0
time="2024-04-18T10:05:29-06:00" level=error msg="unable to cleanup run mounts error in copier subprocess: changing to intended-new-root directory \"$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/33c09cfda165b997aa1b23c70f511b68b389b544feb8559d49836df7fa622aef/merged\": chdir $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/33c09cfda165b997aa1b23c70f511b68b389b544feb8559d49836df7fa622aef/merged: no such file or directory"
time="2024-04-18T10:05:29-06:00" level=error msg="error unmounting container: unmounting build container \"\": layer not known"
Error: server probably quit: unexpected EOF
```

or

```
[1/4] STEP 1/1: FROM alpine AS A
--> 05455a08881e
[2/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS B
[4/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS D
[3/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS C
[4/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 0
[3/4] STEP 2/2: RUN for i in $(seq 0 100); do echo "hello world ${i}" > file-${i}; done
[2/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 1
time="2024-04-18T10:05:05-06:00" level=error msg="unable to cleanup run mounts error in copier subprocess: changing to intended-new-root directory \"$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/800386bdba
bbd9bb7211ff2a0167b91dbb2ec908d263f69b49d358c5532545e9/merged\": chdir $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/800386bdbabbd9bb7211ff2a0167b91dbb2ec908d263f69b49d358c5532545e9/merged: no such file or directory"
time="2024-04-18T10:05:05-06:00" level=error msg="unable to cleanup run mounts error in copier subprocess: changing to intended-new-root directory \"$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/977e6118ddece19faf16468b2963976c4213ce7b154744b49d2d07a05a23aa45/merged\": chdir $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/977e6118ddece19faf16468b2963976c4213ce7b154744b49d2d07a05a23aa45/merged: no such file or
 directory"
Error: building at STEP "RUN exit 1": while running runtime: exit status 1
```

or

```
[1/4] STEP 1/1: FROM alpine AS A
--> 05455a08881e
[3/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS C
[4/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS D
[2/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS B
[2/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 1
[3/4] STEP 2/2: RUN for i in $(seq 0 100); do echo "hello world ${i}" > file-${i}; done
[4/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 0
[4/4] COMMIT
Error: building at STEP "RUN exit 1": while running runtime: exit status 1
```

Depending on which order the RUN clauses execute concurrently in the latest build of Podman (5.0.2).

_For the sake of searchability only for others who may experience this in the future, I first noticed this behavior manifest in podman 4.9 as:_

```
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x55779e551d16]
```

_and ultimately, you may get the error_

```
Aborted (core dumped)
```

_Upgrading to podman 5.0.2 appears to have resolved this nil pointer, but not the filesystem issues with missing image layers._

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

100% of the time, I get no filesystem errors for missing image layers (regardless of which order the `RUN` clauses execute), and I do get the error that `Error: building at STEP "RUN exit 1"` was the real build issue I need to pay attention to:

```
[1/4] STEP 1/1: FROM alpine AS A
--> 05455a08881e
[3/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS C
[4/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS D
[2/4] STEP 1/2: FROM 05455a08881ea9cf0e752bc48e61bbd71a34c029bb13df01e40e3e70e0d007bd AS B
[2/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 1
[3/4] STEP 2/2: RUN for i in $(seq 0 100); do echo "hello world ${i}" > file-${i}; done
[4/4] STEP 2/2: RUN exit 0
[4/4] COMMIT
Error: building at STEP "RUN exit 1": while running runtime: exit status 1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
